title: Lesson 8: Text Formatting (b, i, strong, em)

content.explanation:
✨ Why Text Formatting Matters

When people read websites, they don’t read every word — they scan.

So we use formatting to highlight important parts of text.

🧠 Two Types of Formatting

  • 🎨 Visual tags → change how text looks
  • 📖 Meaning tags → add importance to text
content.syntax_breakdown:
🎨 Visual Formatting Tags

  • <b> → makes text bold (no meaning)
  • <i> → makes text italic (no meaning)

📖 Semantic Formatting Tags

  • <strong> → important text (bold + meaning)
  • <em> → emphasized text (italic + meaning)

⚡ Simple Rule

👉 Use strong/em for real importance
👉 Use b/i only for visual styling

editor_sandbox.starter_code:
System Deployment Notification

Warning: Ensure your configuration parameters are secure before hosting your application codebase.

mini_challenge.instruction:
🎯 Mission: Highlight important text

1. Wrap 'Warning:' with <strong>
2. Wrap 'secure' with <em>
3. Run and observe emphasis changes
mini_challenge.hint:
👉 Example: Warning: secure Both must stay inside the

tag

quiz_engine.question: What is the difference between <b> and <strong>?
options: <b> is visual only, <strong> adds meaning and importance
quiz_engine.explanation_feedback: Correct 🎉 <b> is only visual, while <strong> tells browsers and assistive tools that the text is important.

takeaways: Use semantic tags for better structure and accessibility
